item.title: Docker two-stage build + container hardening
item.intent: Deliver a two-stage Docker build that caches the base image and hardens every container to at least standard security posture by default. Users must never run an unhardened agent.
item.description: BUILD GATE: No Docker build = no running agent. The two-stage architecture matters for user experience: Stage 1 rebuilds rarely (base image), Stage 2 rebuilds fast (custom tools/skills). Hash-based change detection means 'clawhq build' doesn't waste the user's time. Four security postures mean users get hardened-by-default without knowing what cap_drop means. Acceptance: build produces a runnable image; Stage 1 cache hit on no-change; security posture applied without user configuration.
steps: Implement Stage 1 Dockerfile: base OpenClaw + apt packages (cached layer)
Implement Stage 2 Dockerfile: custom tools + skills (fast rebuild layer)
Add hash-based change detection for Stage 1 cache invalidation
Generate build manifest with image hash, layers, size
Implement four security postures: minimal, standard, hardened, paranoid
Apply cap_drop ALL, read-only rootfs, non-root UID 1000, ICC disabled, resource limits per posture
acceptanceCriteria: Build produces a runnable container image
Stage 1 cache hit when base image unchanged
Standard security posture applied by default without user configuration
Build manifest written with image hash and layer info
